task: I need to let my supervisor know I’ll be missing part of a shift and want to send a quick note before things get busy. Can you help me word it clearly so it covers the basics and is easy to send?

prefs.explanation: Her background and values suggest she would respond best to a straightforward, no-frills style. Polished or overly formal wording would likely feel unnecessary to her. She seems more comfortable with language that sounds like a real person talking plainly.

prefs.short: Wants concise answers that get to the point
prefs.explanation: She lives a tired, time-squeezed life, so long-winded help would probably feel like extra work. Short, usable responses fit her practical rhythm better than extended explanations. She likely appreciates enough detail to act, but not a lot of filler.

prefs.short: Likes a calm, respectful, encouraging tone
prefs.explanation: She values kindness and dignity, so harsh or snappy help would clash with her style. A steady, respectful tone would feel more supportive than something flashy or overly emotional. She probably does best with reassurance that still feels grounded.

prefs.short: Prefers clear, simple structure over fancy presentation
prefs.explanation: Her everyday life is practical, so organized answers would likely help her more than dense prose. Bullets or clean steps would make information easier to use quickly. She probably does not want the response to feel cluttered or performative.

prefs.short: Best with plain English, not technical wording
prefs.explanation: She would likely prefer everyday language that feels accessible and direct. Jargon or overly specialized phrasing would create unnecessary friction. Clear, ordinary wording fits her practical, working-class context best.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a short, plain note you can send:

Hi [Supervisor Name], I wanted to let you know I’ll be missing part of my shift today. I’m letting you know ahead of time so you have a heads-up before things get busy. I’ll keep you posted if anything changes.

If you want, I can also make it sound a little more casual or more specific to your situation.

per_pref.reason: Uses plain, unpretentious wording like "short, plain note" and the message itself is straightforward.
Very concise and to the point; it gives a ready-to-send note without extra filler.
Calm and respectful tone throughout, with polite phrasing like "I wanted to let you know" and "heads-up."
Clear simple structure: introduces the note, provides the message, then offers an optional follow-up.
Uses everyday language such as "missing part of my shift" and avoids technical or specialized wording.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a more direct, plain version you can text:

Hi [Supervisor Name], I wanted to let you know I’ll be missing part of my shift today. I’m sending this as early as I can so you have a heads-up. Let me know if you need anything from me.

If you want, I can make it even shorter and more text-like.

per_pref.reason: Uses plain, unpretentious wording like "more direct, plain version" and the sample text is straightforward.
Keeps it concise and usable, with a short template and no filler.
Tone is calm and respectful: "I wanted to let you know" and "Let me know if you need anything from me."
Provides a clean, simple structure with a ready-to-send message and an optional follow-up offer.
Uses everyday language, explicitly avoiding jargon; phrases like "heads-up" and "more text-like" are plain and accessible.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a more text-like version:

Hi [Supervisor Name], just letting you know I’m going to miss part of my shift today. I’m letting you know as early as I can so you have a heads-up.

If you want, I can make it even shorter or a little more casual.

per_pref.reason: Uses plain, unpretentious wording like "just letting you know" and "heads-up"; it sounds natural and straightforward rather than formal.
Very concise and gets to the point quickly: "I’m going to miss part of my shift today" with no extra filler.
Calm, respectful, and encouraging tone throughout, especially "I’m letting you know as early as I can" and "If you want, I can make it even shorter."
Simple structure with a ready-to-send message and an optional follow-up offer. It is clear and usable, though not in bullets or step format.
Uses plain English with no jargon or technical wording; phrases like "miss part of my shift" and "heads-up" are everyday language.
